questions.text: What is the output of: len([1, 2, 3])?
questions.explain: len() returns the number of items in a list.

questions.text: Which method adds an item to the end of a list?
questions.explain: list.append(x) adds x to the end of the list.

questions.text: What does range(3) produce?
questions.explain: range(3) yields 0, 1, 2. The end value is not included.

questions.text: How do you get the last element of a list L?
questions.explain: Negative indices count from the end; -1 is the last.

questions.text: What is the type of {} in Python?
questions.explain: {} is an empty dict; set() is an empty set.

questions.text: Which keyword is used to define a function?
questions.explain: def name(): defines a function in Python.

questions.text: LeetCode Two Sum: best time complexity for a correct solution?
questions.explain: One pass with a hash map: store value and check for complement in O(n).

questions.text: What does list[::-1] do in Python?
questions.explain: Slicing with step -1 returns a reversed copy.

questions.text: Valid palindrome (LeetCode 125): best approach?
questions.explain: Two pointers in O(n) time and O(1) space.

questions.text: How do you copy a list so changes don't affect the original?
questions.explain: .copy() or [:] creates a shallow copy.

questions.text: Contains Duplicate (LeetCode 217): optimal approach?
questions.explain: Set has unique elements; if len(set) < len(list), there's a duplicate. O(n).

questions.text: Best time complexity to insert at the end of a dynamic array?
questions.explain: Append is O(1) amortized; occasional resize is O(n) but rare.

questions.text: Merge Two Sorted Lists (LeetCode 21): required space beyond output?
questions.explain: In-place pointer manipulation uses only O(1) extra space.

questions.text: Reverse a linked list in O(1) extra space?
questions.explain: Iterate with prev, curr, next; reverse pointers one by one.

questions.text: What is the time complexity of 'x in set' for n elements?
questions.explain: Hash set lookup is average O(1).

questions.text: Binary Search (LeetCode 704): time complexity?
questions.explain: Each step halves the search space.

questions.text: Maximum subarray (Kadane): best time complexity?
questions.explain: Kadane's algorithm does a single pass, O(n) time.

questions.text: Implementing a queue with two stacks: enqueue time complexity?
questions.explain: Push to stack 1 is O(1); amortized dequeue is O(1).

questions.text: LRU Cache: which operations must be O(1)?
questions.explain: LRU cache requires O(1) get and put; use hash map + doubly linked list.

questions.text: Detect cycle in linked list (Floyd): space complexity?
questions.explain: Floyd's cycle detection uses two pointers, no extra storage.
